seoTitle: How to View and Print a Sprint Report in Jira
metaDescription: Find, review, and print a Jira sprint report so you can share completed and carried-over work with your team or stakeholders.
intro: After a sprint wraps up, the sprint report gives you a clear picture of what was completed, what got removed, and what rolled over. This guide covers how to open that report in Jira and get a printable version for meetings or records.

steps.heading: Open your Scrum project's reports
steps.body: Go to the project's Reports section from the sidebar, which lists the available report types.

steps.heading: Select Sprint Report
steps.body: Choose Sprint Report from the list and pick the specific sprint you want to review.

steps.heading: Review completed and incomplete work
steps.body: Check the sections showing completed issues, issues not completed, and any work added or removed during the sprint.

steps.heading: Adjust the report view if needed
steps.body: Expand any collapsed sections so all relevant details are visible before printing.

steps.heading: Print or export the report
steps.body: Use your browser's print function, or export the page as a PDF, to create a shareable copy of the sprint report.

faq.question: Where do I find the sprint report if my board doesn't show it?
faq.answer: Sprint reports are only available for Scrum boards; Kanban boards don't generate this report by default.

faq.question: Can I view sprint reports for past, completed sprints?
faq.answer: Yes, the sprint report dropdown lets you select any past sprint associated with the board.

faq.question: Does the sprint report show why issues weren't completed?
faq.answer: It shows which issues weren't completed but doesn't automatically explain why; that context usually comes from your team's notes.

faq.question: Can I export the sprint report as a file instead of printing?
faq.answer: Most browsers let you print to PDF instead of a physical printer, which effectively exports the report as a file.
